chapter_title.ar: باب الْوَقْتِ الَّذِي تَبِينُ مِنْهُ الْمُطَلَّقَةُ وَ الَّذِي يَكُونُ فِيهِ الرَّجْعَةُ مَتَى يَجُوزُ لَهَا أَنْ تَتَزَوَّجَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 26 – The time which she becomes irrevocably divorced from him, and in which there happens to be the reversion, when is it allowed for her to re-marry?

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/150)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/150)
text: الْمُطَلَّقَةُ تَرِثُ وَ تُورَثُ حَتَّى تَرَى الدَّمَ الثَّالِثَ فَإِذَا رَأَتْهُ فَقَدِ انْقَطَعَ .

en.hubeali: And from him, from Safwan, from Ibn Muskan, from Zurara,

(It has been narrated) from one of the two (5th or 6th Imam<sup>asws</sup>) having said: ‘The divorced woman inherits and is inherited (from) until she sees the third blood. So when she sees it, so she is cut-off’.
en.sarwar: 5. It is narrated from the narrator of the previous Hadith from Safwan from ibn Muskan from Zurarah who has said the following: “About the case of a woman who is divorced, one of the two Imam, (abu Ja‘far or abu ‘Abd Allah), ‘Alayhim al-Salam, has said, ‘She inherits and is inherited until she sees blood of Hayd (menses) for the third time after divorce and when that happens it (such relation) is cut off.’”
